original: Steve McPeak rides 101'9" unicycle
Peru announces rediscovered 37m figure of a cat, completed 500 BC to 200 AD, a geoglyph outline in the Nazca Desert
Massachusetts names Podokesaurus holyokensis ("swift-footed lizard of Holyoke") as official state dinosaur
A working human intestine is generated in a laboratory from stem cells in the United States
Scientists from the University of California find evidence that life on Earth may have begun 4.1 billion years ago, 300 million years earlier than previously thought
Alberto Santos-Dumont proves airships are maneuverable by circling the Eiffel Tower in his airship No. 6, winning the Deutsch Prize and 100,000 francs
Hurricane Wilma becomes the most intense Atlantic hurricane on record with a minimum pressure of 882 mb
Streptomycin, the first antibiotic remedy for tuberculosis, is isolated by researchers at Rutgers University in Piscataway, New Jersey
Auguste Lumière, French engineer and filmmaker who made the 1st movie (Workers Leaving Lumière Factory), born in Besançon, France (d. 1954)
US Senate establishesMartin Luther King Jr.Day as a federal holiday, observed on the third Monday in January (on or near his January 15 birthday)
Philip Pullman, English writer known for the trilogy "His Dark Materials", born in Norwich, England
Dystopian novel "Fahrenheit 451" by Ray Bradbury is published in the US
Nicolaas Bloembergen and ArthurSchawlowwin Nobel Prize for Physics (laser)

Philadelphia Phillies right-hander Aaron Nola faces his brother San Diego Padres catcher Austin Nola in first MLB playoff sibling pitcher-batter battle; Austin goes 1-2, with rally starting RBI in 8-5 win at Petco Park, San Diego
Montreal CanadienMaurice "Rocket" Richardbecomes the first NHL player to score 500 goals
First ascent of Cho Oyu, sixth highest mountain in the world at 8,201 metres
Marilyn Bell, Canadian open water swimmer (first to swim across Lake Ontario), born in Toronto, Ontario
Mariner 5 makes fly-by of Venus
Supersonic jet Concorde makes its 1st landing in NYC, a passengerless test flight by Air France, two days after US Supreme Court overrules local authority's ban
US Senate passes a bill curbing ads during children's TV shows
Golden Gate Bridge charges tolls only for southbound cars
"Halloween" film reboot starring Jamie Curtis makes a record $77m for a horror film with a female lead, biggest debut any film with female lead over 55 in US
Napoleon Bonaparteand his Grande Armée begin their retreat from Moscow numbering just 100,000 (started campaign with 500,000)
Battle of Zama: Hannibal Barca and the Carthaginian army are defeated by Roman legions under Scipio Africanus, ending the Second Punic War
Martin Luther King Jr.arrested in Atlanta sit-in
South African anti-apartheid leader Sisulu wins $100,000 Human Rights prize
Mother Teresaof Calcutta is beatified by PopeJohn Paul II
NHL Detroit Red Wing Mickey Redmond scores 1st hat trick against Washington Capitals
Longest NCAA football game (3:52) as Rhode Island beats Maine, 52-30 (6 OT)
1st woman jockey in North America, Anna Lee Wiley in Mexico
Annie Smith Peck, American mountain climber/explorer/diplomat, born in Providence, Rhode Island (d. 1935)

Stirling Mosswins season ending Moroccan Grand Prix at Ain-Diab but fellow Brit Mike Hawthorn takes World Drivers Championship from Moss by just 1 point by finishing second; first British world champion
Bobby Orrmakes his NHL regular-season debut for the Boston Bruins against the Detroit Red Wings
Subrahmanyan Chandrasekhar, India, astrophysicist (Nobel Prize 1983 for work on structure and evolution of the stars), born in Lahore, British India (d. 1995)
Almon Brown Strowger, American undertaker and inventor (automatic telephone-dialing system), born in Penfield, New York (d. 1902)[1]
Yoko Shimomura, Japanese pianist and video game composer, born in Hyogo, Japan
O Yeong-Su, South Korean actor (Squid Game), born in Kaepung County, Japanese Korea
"Dances with Wolves" directed byKevin Costnerand starring Kevin Costner and Mary McDonnell premieres in Washington, D.C. (Academy Awards Best Picture 1991)
Charlotte Brontë's novel "Jane Eyre" is published by Smith, Elder & Co. of London under her pen name "Currer Bell"
Beach Island in North Sea destroyed by a heavy storm flood
Oakland Darryle Lamonica passes for 6 touchdowns vs Buffalo (50-21)
Marguerite Perey, French physicist, discovered the element francium (Fr), born in Villemomble, France (d. 1975)
Thundercat [Stephen Bruner], American bass guitarist (Flying Lotus), born in Los Angeles, California
Peter Tosh [Winston Hubert McIntosh], Jamaican reggae musician (The Wailers -"Get Up Stand Up"; Mystic Man, Legalize It), born in Westmoreland, Jamaica (d. 1987)
Amy Carter, US President Jimmy Carter's daughter and peace activist, born in Plains, Georgia
Walter Bradford Cannon, American physiologist (Wisdom of the Body, coined term "fight or flight response"), born in Prairie du Chien, Wisconsin (d. 1945)
Mordecai "Three Finger" Brown, American Baseball HOF pitcher (World Series 1907, 08; NL wins leader 1909; MLB ERA leader 1906; Chicago Cubs), born in Nyesville, Indiana (d. 1948)
Michael Young, American baseball infielder (7 × MLB All-Star; Gold Glove Award 2008, AL batting champion 2005 Texas Rangers), born in Covina, California
Balbir Singh Sr., Indian field hockey centre forward (Olympic gold 1948, 52, 56) and coach (World Cup gold 1975), born in Haripur Khalsa, India (d. 2020)
Charles Evans, British mountaineer and surgeon (deputy leader 1953 British Everest expedition, led 1955 British Kangchenjunga expedition), born in Liverpool, England (d. 1995)
Atsushi Miyagi, Japanese tennis player (US Open doubles 1955 [with Kosei Kamo] first Japanese players to win a Grand Slam event), born in Tokyo, Japan (d. 2021)
Bart King, American cricket pioneer (North America's greatest all-rounder; helped develop art of swing bowling), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1965)
Salimuzzaman Siddiqui, Pakistani organic chemist and pioneering scientist, born in Subeha, British India (d. 1994)
Bertha Knight Landes, American politician (1st female mayor of any major US city - Mayor of Seattle 1926-28), born in Ware, Massachusetts (d. 1943)
Johnetta Cole, American educator, president of Spellman College, President National Council of Negro Women, born in Jacksonville, Florida
Jennifer Holliday, American Grammy and Tony Award-winning stage actress, and pop, R&B, and gospel singer (Dream Girls), born in Houston, Texas

Brad Daugherty, American basketball center (5 x NBA All-Star; Cleveland Cavaliers), NASCAR team owner (JTG Daugherty Racing, winner Daytona 500 2023) and broadcaster (ESPN, NBC, CBS), born in Black Mountain, North Carolina
Pras [Prakazrel Michel], Haitian-American rapper, songwriter and producer (The Fugees - "Killing Me Softly"), born in Brooklyn, New York City
Gillian Jacobs, American actress known for "Community" and "The Bear", born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
SpotEmGottem [Nehemiah Harden], American rapper and songwriter ("Beat Box"), born in Jacksonville, Florida
